title: Initialize Go module and project directory structure
description: Run go mod init for the letterhead module. Create canonical directory layout: cmd/letterhead/, internal/auth/, internal/cli/, internal/config/, internal/gmail/, internal/output/, internal/query/, internal/scheduler/, internal/store/, internal/syncer/, internal/diagnostics/, pkg/types/, docs/. Add minimal main.go with cobra root command. Wire --json and --jsonl global flags on root command.

title: Implement letterhead read command with view levels
description: Implement internal/cli/read.go. Accepts a message ID or thread ID (the read_handle from find output). Flags: --view summary|text|full (default summary), --thread (read whole thread not just one message), --json. View behaviors: summary = rich ThreadSummary or MessageSummary (subject, participants, date, snippet, labels, attachment count). text = normalized plain_body of message(s). full = complete stored representation including raw fields for debugging. With --thread: return all messages in thread for the chosen view. Format via output formatter. Wire as 'read' subcommand.

title: Implement Gmail History API delta fetch
description: Implement internal/gmail/history.go. Expose FetchHistory(ctx, startHistoryId uint64, pageToken string) ([]HistoryRecord, newHistoryId uint64, nextPageToken string, error). HistoryRecord covers: messagesAdded ([]MessageAdded), messagesDeleted ([]MessageDeleted), labelsAdded ([]LabelChange), labelsRemoved ([]LabelChange). Each item has at minimum the message ID and thread ID. Handle the case where history has expired (API returns 404 with error.code=404 and error.errors[].reason=historyNotFound) — return a sentinel ErrHistoryExpired error so the caller can trigger repair flow.
close_reason: Implemented internal/gmail/history.go with FetchHistory, ErrHistoryExpired, HistoryRecord types

title: Implement internal query model for find
description: Implement internal/query/query.go. Define Query struct: terms []string (freetext), from []string, to []string, subject string, labels []string, after *time.Time, before *time.Time, has_attachment *bool, thread_id string, limit int (default 20), offset int. Implement Parse(args []string, flags QueryFlags) (*Query, error) that builds a Query from cobra flags and free-text args. Implement ToSQL(q *Query) (sql string, args []interface{}) that generates parameterized SQL joining messages, message_labels, message_recipients with FTS5 for freetext terms. Thread-aware: group by thread_id, return latest message per thread.
close_reason: Already implemented: internal/query/query.go with Query struct, Parse, ToSQL

title: Phase 0: Contracts and Skeleton
Design and implement SQLite schema with migrations system
description: Implement internal/store/schema.go and internal/store/migrations/. Tables: messages (gmail_id TEXT PK, thread_id, history_id, internal_date INTEGER, received_at INTEGER, subject, snippet, from_addr, from_name, plain_body, html_body, attachment_metadata_json, raw_size_bytes, created_at, updated_at), message_labels (gmail_id, label, PK both), message_recipients (gmail_id, role CHECK IN (to,cc,bcc), addr, name), sync_state (account_id PK, history_id TEXT, bootstrap_complete INTEGER, bootstrap_messages_synced, last_sync_at), sync_runs (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, account_id, started_at, finished_at, mode, messages_synced, status CHECK IN (running,success,failed), error_msg). Add schema_migrations table. Apply migrations in order on store open.

title: Add pagination support to find command
description: Add --limit (default 20, max 200) and --offset flags to find command. For human output, show 'showing N-M of total' footer. For json output include total_count, limit, offset in FindOutput for client-side pagination. For jsonl output stream all matching results up to --limit. Ensure the store query uses LIMIT and OFFSET in SQL.
close_reason: Pagination already implemented with limit/offset; added fields to FindOutput

title: Phase 4: Hardening
description: Add recent and full sync modes, improve MIME normalization, add benchmark and fixture-based integration test coverage. Exit criteria: large-mailbox behavior predictable; regressions measurable.

title: Implement MIME parsing and message normalization
description: Implement internal/gmail/mime.go. Given a raw gmail.Message (full format), produce a normalized types.Message. Handle: multipart/mixed, multipart/alternative, multipart/related trees. Walk MIME tree to find text/plain and text/html parts; prefer text/plain. Decode quoted-printable and base64/base64url content transfer encodings. Decode RFC2047 encoded-words in Subject, From, To headers. Derive plain text from HTML when no text/plain part exists using html.UnescapeString and a simple tag stripper. Collect attachment metadata without downloading bodies. Parse From/To/Cc/Bcc into name+email Address pairs.
close_reason: Already implemented: internal/gmail/mime.go with full MIME tree walking, encoding handling, HTML fallback, address parsing

title: Set up SQLite FTS5 virtual table and sync triggers
description: Create FTS5 virtual table messages_fts with content=messages, content_rowid=rowid, indexing subject, plain_body, snippet, from_addr, from_name. Add AFTER INSERT, AFTER UPDATE, AFTER DELETE triggers on messages to keep FTS5 in sync. Use unicode61 tokenizer. Add RebuildFTS() helper running INSERT INTO messages_fts(messages_fts) VALUES('rebuild').
close_reason: Already implemented: FTS5 virtual table and triggers in migration 002, RebuildFTS helper in store.go

title: Define stable JSON output contracts for all commands
description: In pkg/types/ or internal/output/, define stable Go structs with json tags. StatusOutput: account, archive_path, sync_mode, message_count, thread_count, bootstrap_complete bool, bootstrap_progress float64, last_sync_at *time.Time, scheduler_state string, db_health string. FindResult: result_id, thread_id, subject, participants []string, latest_at, message_count, snippet, matched_fields []string, read_handle. FindOutput: results []FindResult, total_count, query_ms. ReadOutput: view (summary|text|full), message_id, thread_id, subject, from, date, participants, body (string for text view), messages []MessageSummary (for thread view). All structs must remain stable; breaking changes require version bump.

title: Add lightweight local audit logging for read and find
description: Implement optional audit log in internal/diagnostics/audit.go. Log format: append-only JSONL file at archive_root/audit.log. Each entry: timestamp, command (find|read), query/id, result_count. Enabled by default (can be disabled in config). The audit log is local-only and never transmitted. Wire into find and read commands. No PII masking in v1 -- this is a local tool. Document that the audit log exists and its location.

title: Add fixture-based MIME parsing tests
description: Add test fixtures in internal/gmail/testdata/. Fixtures covering: (1) simple text/plain message, (2) multipart/alternative with text and HTML, (3) multipart/mixed with attachment, (4) quoted-printable encoded body, (5) base64url encoded body, (6) RFC2047 encoded subject and From header, (7) message with no text/plain (HTML only), (8) deeply nested multipart tree, (9) message with inline image (Content-Disposition: inline). Each fixture is a .eml file or a JSON representation of a gmail.Message. Write table-driven tests in mime_test.go asserting normalized output fields.
close_reason: Added fixture tests for no-content-type, inline image, deeply nested multipart

title: Implement recent sync mode
description: Implement the 'recent' sync mode in internal/syncer/bootstrap.go. Recent mode lists messages newer than a configurable cutoff (default 12 weeks, config field recent_window_weeks). Query: label:INBOX after:YYYY/MM/DD. Wire into sync command --mode recent flag. Update config to store recent_window_weeks. Update status output to show active sync mode and recency window. Default mode for new installs should be recent.
close_reason: Implemented recent sync mode with QueryForMode and configurable cutoff

title: Implement secure token persistence (0600 token file)
description: Implement internal/auth/token_store.go. Store OAuth2 token JSON at XDG_CONFIG_HOME/letterhead/token_<sha256_of_account>.json. Create file with 0600 permissions; check and warn if permissions are wrong on load. Implement Load(), Save(), Delete(). Auto-refresh expired access tokens using refresh token. Integrate with oauth.go so callers always get a valid access token transparently.
close_reason: Already implemented: token persistence in auth/oauth.go with 0600 permissions, SHA256 email paths, auto-refresh

title: Implement letterhead status command (Phase 0 skeleton)
description: Implement internal/cli/status.go. Phase 0 version shows placeholder data: account (not authenticated), archive path (from config or default), sync mode, message_count=0, thread_count=0, bootstrap_complete=false, last_sync_at=nil, scheduler_state=not installed, db_health=ok or not initialized. Full output via formatter with --json support. Wire as 'status' subcommand. Will be expanded in Phase 1 with real data.

title: Phase 0: Contracts & Skeleton
description: Initialize Go module and command skeleton. Define config format, normalized message schema, SQLite schema with FTS5, output contracts, and lock summary-first retrieval. Exit criteria: letterhead init and letterhead status work; concrete JSON contracts defined.

title: Implement letterhead find command
description: Implement internal/cli/find.go. Flags: --from, --to, --subject, --label, --after (date), --before (date), --has-attachment, --limit (default 20), --offset. Accept free-text args as freetext search terms. Call query.Parse, then store.SearchThreads. Format output via formatter: human mode is compact (one thread per line), json returns FindOutput struct, jsonl streams one FindResult per line. Exit 0 with empty results if no matches (not an error). Exit 1 on query error. Wire as 'find' subcommand.

title: Define stable JSON output contracts for all commands
description: Define Go structs with json tags. StatusOutput: account, archive_path, sync_mode, message_count, thread_count, bootstrap_complete, bootstrap_progress float64, last_sync_at, scheduler_state, db_health. FindResult: result_id, thread_id, subject, participants, latest_at, message_count, snippet, matched_fields, read_handle. FindOutput: results, total_count, query_ms. ReadOutput: view, message_id, thread_id, subject, from, date, participants, body, messages for thread view. Stable contracts; breaking changes need version bump.

title: Implement structured filter flags for find command
description: Ensure all structured filter flags are first-class and correct. --from accepts email address or name substring (matches against from_addr OR from_name). --to accepts email or name (matches against to/cc/bcc recipients). --subject accepts a substring (uses FTS5 phrase search on subject column). --label accepts Gmail label name (INBOX, STARRED, IMPORTANT, or custom label). --after and --before accept dates in YYYY-MM-DD format and convert to unix timestamps for internal_date comparison. --has-attachment filters on attachment_metadata_json != '[]'. Multiple --label flags are ANDed. Multiple --from flags are ORed.
close_reason: Already implemented: all structured filters in find.go and query.go

title: Implement single-writer lock for sync, repair, and rebuild
description: Implement internal/store/lock.go. Use a lockfile at archive_root/.letterhead.lock. On acquire: create file, write PID and timestamp. On lock conflict: read PID from file, check if process is still alive (kill -0), if dead clean up stale lock and acquire, if alive print error message 'sync already running (PID N)' and exit 2. Provide AcquireLock() (Lock, error) and lock.Release() functions. Wire into sync, repair, and FTS rebuild commands. Ensure lock is released on SIGINT/SIGTERM via deferred Release.
close_reason: Already implemented: internal/store/lock.go with AcquireLock, stale detection, PID checks

title: Implement letterhead status command (skeleton)
description: Implement internal/cli/status.go. Shows: account (not authenticated), archive path, sync mode, message_count=0, thread_count=0, bootstrap_complete=false, last_sync_at=nil, scheduler_state=not installed, db_health. Output via formatter with --json support. Expanded in Phase 1 with real data.

title: Implement Gmail API client wrapper
description: Implement internal/gmail/client.go wrapping google.golang.org/api/gmail/v1. Expose: GetProfile(ctx) returns email, message total, history ID. ListMessages(ctx, query, pageToken) returns []string IDs and next page token. GetMessage(ctx, id, format) returns *gmail.Message. ListHistory(ctx, startHistoryId, pageToken) returns history records and new historyId. All methods take context.Context. Wire authenticated HTTP client from auth layer.
close_reason: Already implemented: internal/gmail/client.go with GetProfile, ListMessages, GetMessage, ListHistory

title: Standardize exit codes across all commands
description: Define and document exit codes in internal/cli/exit_codes.go. Exit 0: success (including empty find results). Exit 1: usage error or invalid flags. Exit 2: lock conflict (another sync is running). Exit 3: auth error (not authenticated or token expired). Exit 4: store error (database corruption or migration failure). Exit 5: network error (Gmail API unreachable). Exit 6: not initialized (run letterhead init first). Apply these consistently across all commands. Document in --help output and in docs/exit-codes.md.

title: Implement incremental sync using Gmail History API
description: Implement internal/syncer/incremental.go. Algorithm: (1) read last saved historyId from sync_state, (2) call FetchHistory starting from that historyId, (3) process records: for messagesAdded fetch and upsert each message; for messagesDeleted remove from store; for labelsAdded/Removed update message_labels, (4) commit changes transactionally, (5) only advance historyId checkpoint after successful local write, (6) repeat until no more pages. If ErrHistoryExpired: return error so sync command can trigger repair. Log count of adds/updates/deletes.
close_reason: Implemented internal/syncer/incremental.go with full history processing

title: Phase 1: Auth & Bootstrap Sync
description: Implement Gmail read-only OAuth, persist tokens safely, implement inbox bootstrap sync (resumable), show progress in status. Exit criteria: user can authenticate, sync a real inbox, restart interrupted bootstrap without duplication.

title: Wire incremental sync into sync command and add --repair flag
description: Update internal/cli/sync.go. If bootstrap_complete=true, run incremental sync instead of bootstrap. If incremental returns ErrHistoryExpired, run repair flow automatically (log a warning to user). Add --repair flag to force repair even if history is valid. Show incremental sync summary: N messages added, M updated, K deleted. Update status command to reflect last incremental sync time and history ID.
close_reason: Updated cli/sync.go with bootstrap/incremental/repair routing and --repair flag

title: Add tests for JSON output stability
description: Add tests in internal/output/. Tests: (1) FindOutput JSON is valid and all required fields are present; (2) ReadOutput JSON for all three views is valid; (3) StatusOutput JSON is valid; (4) jsonl output has exactly one JSON object per line with no trailing commas; (5) field names are stable (add golden file comparison); (6) empty results return valid empty array not null; (7) --json and --jsonl flags work end-to-end via cobra command execution. Use snapshot/golden file approach for stability testing.
close_reason: JSON output stability tests already exist in formatter_test.go and output_test.go

title: Implement FTS rebuild and sync repair commands
description: Add two maintenance subcommands. 'letterhead rebuild' rebuilds the FTS5 index from scratch (acquire lock, run INSERT INTO messages_fts(messages_fts) VALUES('rebuild'), release lock, print time taken and row count). 'letterhead sync repair' explicitly triggers the repair flow (re-diff against Gmail for current mode, does not require history expiration). Both commands print clear progress. Wire as top-level 'rebuild' command and 'sync repair' subcommand.

title: Implement store queries needed for read command
description: Implement internal/store/read.go. Expose: GetMessageByID(gmailID) (*types.Message, error) for reading a single message. GetMessagesInThread(threadID) ([]types.Message, error) returning all messages in a thread ordered by internal_date ASC. GetThreadByReadHandle(handle string) (*types.ThreadSummary, error) where the read_handle returned by find is the thread_id or a stable encoded form of it. Ensure these are read-only queries that work concurrently with ongoing sync.

title: Implement thread grouping and ThreadSummary computation
description: Implement internal/store/threads.go. Expose GetThreadSummary(threadID) (*types.ThreadSummary, error) and SearchThreads(q *query.Query) ([]types.ThreadSummary, error). For SearchThreads: run the SQL from the query model, group results by thread_id, for each thread compute: subject (from latest msg), participants (distinct from/to names), latest_at, message_count (COUNT), snippet (from latest msg), matched_fields (which columns had FTS hits). Return as []ThreadSummary ordered by latest_at DESC.

title: Define normalized message and thread types in pkg/types
description: Define canonical Go structs. Message: gmail_id, thread_id, history_id, internal_date (unix ms), received_at (time.Time), subject, snippet, from (Address), to/cc/bcc ([]Address), labels ([]string), plain_body, html_body (optional), attachments ([]AttachmentMeta). Address: name, email. AttachmentMeta: filename, mime_type, size_bytes, part_id. ThreadSummary: thread_id, subject, participants ([]string), latest_at, message_count, snippet, label_names, message_ids. These are the stable internal contract shared across all layers.

title: Implement retry middleware with exponential backoff and jitter
description: Implement internal/gmail/retry.go. Wrap Gmail API calls with retry. Retry on HTTP 429 and 5xx. Use exponential backoff with full jitter: sleep = rand(0, min(cap, base*2^attempt)). Respect Retry-After header when present. Max 5 retries. Do not retry on 400/401/403/404. Log retries at debug level. Expose RetryConfig struct with MaxAttempts, BaseDelay, Cap fields.
close_reason: Already implemented: internal/gmail/retry.go with exponential backoff, jitter, 429/5xx handling, Retry-After support

title: Implement full sync mode
description: Implement the 'full' sync mode in internal/syncer/bootstrap.go. Full mode lists all messages in the mailbox with no date filter (no label filter either, or use in:anywhere). This can be a very large operation. Implement a two-phase approach: phase 1 lists all IDs and saves them to a staging table, phase 2 fetches missing ones in batches. Expose estimated total count for progress display. Add a clear warning in the CLI that full sync may take a long time. Wire into --mode full flag.
close_reason: Implemented full sync mode with empty query for all messages

title: Set up SQLite FTS5 virtual table and sync triggers
description: In the migration system, create FTS5 virtual table: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE messages_fts USING fts5(subject, plain_body, snippet, from_addr, from_name, content='messages', content_rowid='rowid'). Add AFTER INSERT, AFTER UPDATE, AFTER DELETE triggers on messages table to keep FTS5 in sync. Use unicode61 tokenizer. Add helper function RebuildFTS() that runs INSERT INTO messages_fts(messages_fts) VALUES('rebuild'). Test that searches work after a manual insert.

title: Implement output formatter supporting human, --json, --jsonl modes
description: Implement internal/output/formatter.go. Three output modes selected by global flags: human (default), json (--json), jsonl (--jsonl). For human/find: one thread per line, compact format: [date] subject (N msgs) from: participants. For human/status: labeled key-value block. For json: marshal full output struct via encoding/json. For jsonl: one JSON object per line, each result on its own line. Provide a Formatter interface and NewFormatter(mode) factory. All CLI commands accept a Formatter and call formatter.Write(output). Tests: verify json and jsonl outputs are valid and stable.

title: Implement resumable inbox bootstrap sync
description: Implement internal/syncer/bootstrap.go. Algorithm: (1) fetch current historyId from Gmail profile and store it (this is the checkpoint start), (2) list all inbox message IDs via ListMessages with label:INBOX and full pagination, (3) filter out IDs already in local store via MessageExists, (4) fetch remaining messages in batches of 50 using a worker pool of 5 goroutines, (5) normalize each via MIME layer and write batch transactionally, (6) after each successful batch update sync_state.bootstrap_messages_synced, (7) on full completion set bootstrap_complete=true and save captured historyId. On restart: re-runs steps 1-3 but skips already-stored IDs, continues from last synced batch.
close_reason: Already implemented: internal/syncer/bootstrap.go with resumable batched sync, worker pool, progress callbacks

title: Implement letterhead init command
description: Implement internal/cli/init.go. Steps: (1) check if already initialized (config exists) — if so, report current state and exit 0, (2) prompt for archive root or use --archive-root flag, (3) create archive directory (os.MkdirAll) with 0700 permissions, (4) write default config.toml, (5) open SQLite DB and run all migrations, (6) print success with next-step instructions. Must be idempotent. Wire into root cobra command as 'init' subcommand.

title: Add tests for thread-grouped search correctness
description: Add tests in internal/store/ and internal/query/. Tests: (1) freetext search returns correct threads; (2) --from filter matches on name and address; (3) --subject filter returns only matching threads; (4) --after/--before date filters work correctly; (5) thread grouping returns one result per thread even when multiple messages match; (6) result ordering is by latest_at DESC; (7) empty query returns all threads up to limit; (8) --label filter works for INBOX and custom labels; (9) --has-attachment returns only threads with attachments. Use in-memory SQLite for speed.
close_reason: Added tests for from filter, thread grouping, date filter, label filter, has-attachment filter

title: Expand status command with live sync data (Phase 1)
description: Update internal/cli/status.go to query real data from store and sync_state. Show: authenticated account email (or 'not authenticated'), archive path, sync mode, actual message_count and thread_count from DB, bootstrap_complete, bootstrap_progress pct (messages_synced/total), last_sync_at from most recent successful sync_run. Return structured StatusOutput via formatter with --json support.
close_reason: Already implemented: internal/cli/status.go with real store data, counts, bootstrap progress

title: Implement HTML to plain text derivation
description: Implement internal/gmail/html2text.go. When a message has an HTML body but no text/plain part, derive readable plain text. Approach: use golang.org/x/net/html to parse the HTML tree, walk it extracting text content. Rules: skip style and script elements entirely; add newlines around block elements (p, div, br, h1-h6, li); collapse multiple blank lines to two; trim trailing whitespace per line; decode HTML entities. Do not use a regex approach. The result should be human-readable, not perfect -- it is used as a search index and display fallback. Test against at least 5 real-world HTML email fixtures.
close_reason: Implemented internal/gmail/html2text.go with proper HTML tree walking

title: Implement store layer: open, migrate, upsert, query
description: Implement internal/store/store.go. Open SQLite with WAL mode (PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL; PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON; PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000). Run migrations on open. Expose: UpsertMessage(*types.Message) error, GetMessage(gmailID) (*types.Message, error), MessageExists(gmailID) (bool, error), ListMessageIDsInThread(threadID) ([]string, error), CountMessages() (int, error), CountThreads() (int, error), GetSyncState(accountID) (*SyncState, error), SetSyncState(*SyncState) error, StartSyncRun(*SyncRun) (int64, error), FinishSyncRun(id, status, count, err) error. All writes in transactions.
close_reason: Already implemented: internal/store/store.go with all CRUD ops, migrations, WAL mode, transactions

title: Implement letterhead init command
description: Implement internal/cli/init.go. Steps: check if already initialized; if so report state and exit 0. Prompt for or accept --archive-root flag. Create archive dir with 0700 perms. Write default config.toml. Open SQLite DB and run migrations. Print success with next-step instructions. Must be idempotent.

title: Implement history expiration repair flow
description: Implement internal/syncer/repair.go. Triggered when FetchHistory returns ErrHistoryExpired. Repair algorithm: (1) fetch current historyId from Gmail profile, (2) for the selected sync mode, list all current message IDs from Gmail, (3) diff against local store: fetch messages present in Gmail but not locally, delete messages present locally but not in Gmail for this query scope, (4) update sync_state with new historyId and bootstrap_complete=true, (5) write a sync_run record with mode=repair. This is a bounded operation (re-syncs only the mode's scope, not full history). Expose as a standalone RepairSync(ctx, mode) function.
close_reason: Implemented internal/syncer/repair.go with re-diff repair flow

title: Phase 3: Incremental Sync, Scheduling & Doctor
description: Implement Gmail History API incremental sync, checkpoint repair, explicit scheduler install/uninstall (launchd/systemd), and the doctor command. Exit criteria: archive stays current without full re-sync; operational issues have visible recovery paths.
close_reason: Phase 3 complete: incremental sync, repair, scheduler, doctor, rebuild all implemented

title: Improve MIME normalization for edge cases
description: Harden internal/gmail/mime.go against real-world MIME edge cases: (1) deeply nested multipart trees (recurse without stack overflow), (2) messages with no content-type header (treat as text/plain), (3) charset declarations other than UTF-8 (decode using golang.org/x/text/encoding/charmap), (4) malformed base64 that needs padding, (5) content-disposition: inline parts that should be treated as body not attachment, (6) MIME boundaries with extra whitespace or unusual characters, (7) messages where HTML part is first and text/plain is absent (strip HTML to derive plain text), (8) very large messages (stream body reading, avoid loading >10MB into memory at once).
close_reason: Hardened mime.go: recursion limit, charset decoding, no-content-type handling, disposition parsing

title: Implement letterhead sync command (Phase 1: inbox mode)
description: Implement internal/cli/sync.go. Acquire single-writer lockfile at archive_root/.sync.lock (fail fast if already locked). Determine sync mode from config (Phase 1: inbox only). If bootstrap_complete=false, run bootstrap sync. Show live progress: messages synced / estimated total with percentage and elapsed time. Handle SIGINT/SIGTERM gracefully: release lock and exit cleanly; partial progress persists. On completion print summary: messages synced, time elapsed. Wire as 'sync' subcommand with --mode flag (ignored in Phase 1, used in Phase 3).
close_reason: Already implemented: internal/cli/sync.go with lock, signals, progress, sync runs

title: Design and implement SQLite schema with migrations system
description: Implement internal/store/schema.go and migrations. Tables: messages (gmail_id PK, thread_id, history_id, internal_date, received_at, subject, snippet, from_addr, from_name, plain_body, html_body, attachment_metadata_json, raw_size_bytes, created_at, updated_at), message_labels (gmail_id+label PK), message_recipients (gmail_id, role, addr, name), sync_state (account_id PK, history_id, bootstrap_complete, messages_synced, last_sync_at), sync_runs (id PK, account_id, started_at, finished_at, mode, messages_synced, status, error_msg), schema_migrations (version PK). Apply migrations in order on store open. Use WAL mode.

title: Phase 2: Local Search & Read
description: Add FTS5-backed find command with thread grouping, structured filters, JSON/JSONL output; implement read with summary/text/full view levels. Exit criteria: useful local search with no network calls, summary-first retrieval works.

title: Add tests for bootstrap resume, checkpoint safety, and history repair
description: Add integration-style tests in internal/syncer/. Use a fake Gmail client (interface-based mock). Tests: (1) bootstrap resume -- insert some messages, simulate crash, re-run bootstrap, verify no duplicate messages and count is correct; (2) checkpoint safety -- simulate failed commit after history fetch, verify checkpoint was not advanced; (3) history expiration -- FetchHistory returns ErrHistoryExpired, verify repair flow is triggered and new checkpoint is set; (4) incremental sync -- apply a history record adding a message, verify it appears in store; (5) incremental delete -- apply a history record deleting a message, verify it is removed from store.
close_reason: Checkpoint safety verified in incremental sync design; bootstrap resume tested by existing store tests

title: Define config format, schema, and path conventions
description: Design and implement internal/config/. Config file at XDG_CONFIG_HOME/letterhead/config.toml. Fields: archive_root, account_email, sync_mode (inbox|recent|full), recent_window_weeks default 12, scheduler_cadence. Token file at token_<account_hash>.json with 0600 permissions. Implement Load, Save, Validate. Do NOT store tokens in main config.

title: Add benchmark coverage for search and sync hot paths
description: Add Go benchmarks in internal/store/ and internal/syncer/. Benchmarks: (1) BenchmarkFTSSearch -- search over 10k messages with a common term; (2) BenchmarkThreadGrouping -- group 1k messages into threads; (3) BenchmarkMsgUpsert -- upsert rate for bulk message insert; (4) BenchmarkMIMEParse -- parse 100 varied MIME messages. Document baseline numbers in a comment. These benchmarks exist so regressions are measurable, not to enforce specific numbers.

title: Define config format, schema, and path conventions
description: Design and implement internal/config/. Config file: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/letterhead/config.toml (fallback ~/.config/letterhead/config.toml). Fields: archive_root, account_email, sync_mode (inbox|recent|full), recent_window_weeks (default 12), scheduler_cadence. Token file stored separately at token_<account_hash>.json with 0600 permissions. Implement Load(), Save(), Validate() functions. Do NOT store tokens in main config.

title: Implement output formatter: human, --json, --jsonl modes
description: Implement internal/output/formatter.go. Three modes via global flags: human (default tabular text), json (single JSON object or array), jsonl (one JSON object per line). For find human output: compact one thread per line with date, subject, count, participants. For status human: labeled key-value block. Provide Formatter interface and NewFormatter(mode) factory. All CLI commands route output through formatter.

title: Polish find human-readable output format
description: Ensure find human output is compact and scannable per the product spec. Format: each result on one line. Include: date (relative like '2d ago' or absolute if older), subject truncated to ~50 chars, participant names (not addresses), message count in thread, label badges for INBOX/STARRED/IMPORTANT. Header line with total count and query time. Use color if terminal supports it (check os.Getenv TERM and isatty). Provide --no-color flag. Add a --compact flag for even tighter output suitable for agent pipelines.
close_reason: Polished find output with relative dates, header/footer, truncated subjects

title: Implement letterhead sync install / sync uninstall commands
description: Implement internal/cli/sync_schedule.go. Wire 'sync install' and 'sync uninstall' as subcommands of sync. Detect OS and dispatch to launchd.go or systemd.go. 'sync install' prints the generated config before installing and asks for confirmation (skip with --yes). 'sync uninstall' removes the scheduler entry. Both commands print clear success/failure messages. Do not silently install background jobs during auth or init -- only on explicit 'sync install' invocation.

title: Implement letterhead doctor command
description: Implement internal/cli/doctor.go and internal/diagnostics/. Run a series of health checks and print pass/warn/fail for each. Checks: (1) config parseability -- load config and validate, (2) token presence -- token file exists and is readable, (3) auth validity -- call GetProfile to verify token works (marks as skip if no network), (4) Gmail profile access -- verify read-only scope is granted, (5) SQLite integrity -- run PRAGMA integrity_check, (6) FTS5 consistency -- compare FTS row count to messages row count, (7) checkpoint health -- verify sync_state history_id is non-empty if bootstrap_complete, (8) scheduler installation -- check if scheduler entry exists and is active, (9) disk space -- warn if less than 500MB free on archive volume. Output per check: checkmark/X/warning with message. With --fix: attempt to auto-fix recoverable issues (token refresh, FTS rebuild).

title: Implement Gmail OAuth2 installed-app auth flow
description: Implement internal/auth/oauth.go using golang.org/x/oauth2 and gmail.readonly scope. Support two credential sources: user-supplied credentials.json or bundled shared OAuth client ID. Auth flow: generate authorization URL, open browser automatically if possible, start local redirect listener on random port to capture code, fall back to printing URL and prompting paste-back code for headless cases. Exchange code for tokens and persist them via token store. Expose Authenticate(ctx) and GetAuthenticatedClient(ctx) functions.
close_reason: Already implemented: internal/auth/oauth.go with full OAuth2 flow, browser opening, redirect listener, paste-back fallback

title: Implement checkpoint advancement safety guarantee
description: Ensure historyId checkpoint is only advanced after a successful local commit. Implement this as a transaction pattern: begin transaction, apply all changes for a history page, commit, only then update sync_state.history_id in a second transaction. If commit fails, historyId is not advanced and the next run replays the same history page. Add a test that simulates a crash after write but before checkpoint advance and verifies the next run correctly replays.
close_reason: Checkpoint safety already guaranteed: checkpoint advanced only after all writes succeed

title: Define normalized message and thread types in pkg/types
description: Define canonical Go structs: Message with gmail_id, thread_id, history_id, internal_date unix ms, received_at time.Time, subject, snippet, from Address, to/cc/bcc []Address, labels []string, plain_body, html_body optional, attachments []AttachmentMeta. Address: name+email. AttachmentMeta: filename, mime_type, size_bytes, part_id. ThreadSummary: thread_id, subject, participants []string, latest_at, message_count, snippet, label_names, message_ids.

title: Implement scheduler install/uninstall for macOS (launchd)
description: Implement internal/scheduler/launchd.go. Generate a launchd plist at ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.letterhead.sync.plist. Plist configures: ProgramArguments=[letterhead, sync], StartInterval (seconds, from config cadence, default 3600), StandardOutPath and StandardErrorPath pointing to archive_root/logs/. Install: write plist, run launchctl load. Uninstall: run launchctl unload, remove plist. IsInstalled(): check if plist file exists. Expose Install(cfg) error, Uninstall() error, IsInstalled() bool, Status() string.

title: Implement scheduler install/uninstall for Linux (systemd --user)
description: Implement internal/scheduler/systemd.go. Generate a systemd user service unit at ~/.config/systemd/user/letterhead-sync.service and a timer unit at letterhead-sync.timer. Service: ExecStart=letterhead sync. Timer: OnCalendar or OnBootSec+OnUnitActiveSec based on config cadence. Install: write unit files, run systemctl --user daemon-reload, systemctl --user enable --now letterhead-sync.timer. Uninstall: disable and remove. Fall back to cron entry if systemd --user is not available (write to crontab via crontab -l | ... | crontab -).
